metric_value: Retainer-based interactive program providing one-on-one access to an Analyst or team
Core Access Deliverables (CAD): free for all active clients; ad hoc analyst access + e-mail/phone + trade show dialogue + Impacts/Profiles/White Papers
Six-Month Introductory AAS: $6,000; invoiced on signing; one-time only for first-time clients
Twelve-Month Multi-Day AAS: $7,000/year + per-diem analyst rate; min 2 consulting days; invoiced quarterly

notes: Matches Kastner's Group VP title era

metric_value: +$2,000 MAS service add-on (20% discount) when AAS bundled with practice MAS research
Forrester/Gartner = subscription service; Aberdeen = personal service putting research into client's exact context
Six-Month Introductory AAS renews free if client commits to: consulting project, MAS service ($22,500), publication/amplification project ($15K+), or RAMP/Strategy Acceleration/custom research
AAS is the desired entry product on which other paid engagements are built
Analysts should pitch AAS at close of research briefing
Key qualifying Qs: talking with Gartner/Forrester? marketing budget? staff size? funding/M&A/IPO activity?
Objection 'I don't have any money' — $500/month for 6 months is discretionary; access plus half-day consulting
Objection 'working with Gartner/Forrester' — 'we don't do what they do; they do subscription, we put research in your exact context'
Objection 'just write me a Profile' — 'A Profile is market amplification; AAS is building/buffing/polishing the go-to-market strategy'
'More than a decade' / 'over thirteen years' of AAS track record (dates doc ~2001)
